title: microcephala lamellata subsp. lamellata
intro: Microcephala lamellata subsp. lamellata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of fern. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Microcephala lamellata subsp. lamellata is often made into a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
